skill_spec.name: collaboration-issue-distillation
skill_spec.summary: Distill a bounded collaboration subject into separate canonical evidence, distilled findings, and publish/build readiness artifacts before any publish or build gate proceeds.
skill_spec.why_it_matters: Issue review should train the operator through explicit, rebuildable artifacts instead of hidden prompt sprawl.

required_inputs.name: subject_locator
required_inputs.description: Stable locator for the bounded collaboration subject. For the GitHub adapter this is the issue URL; the core contract is not GitHub-specific.

required_inputs.name: subject_memory
required_inputs.description: Provider-backed subject memory carrying canonical evidence refs and any adapter snapshot needed to replay the bounded subject.

required_inputs.name: publication_target
required_inputs.description: Requested publish/build surface and approval semantics for the distilled output.

adapter_surfaces.name: github_issue_thread
adapter_surfaces.role: Uses Issue #110 as the living ledger for the GitHub adapter.

adapter_surfaces.name: chat_or_cli_agent_context
adapter_surfaces.role: Supports the same contract outside GitHub while preserving the same approval semantics.
canonical_evidence: bounded transcript or session records carried in subject_memory

outputs.name: canonical_evidence
outputs.description: Canonical evidence manifest listing the bounded evidence refs used for this distillation.

outputs.name: distilled_findings
outputs.description: Maintainer recap and distilled rationale derived from canonical evidence.

outputs.name: publish_build_readiness
outputs.description: Readiness and authorization status for any publish/build step, kept separate from evidence and findings.

governance.canonical_evidence_rule: Issue comments and GitHub Actions receipts are canonical evidence for the GitHub adapter; derived memory must remain rebuildable and subordinate.
governance.approval_boundary: Any public-facing draft PR refresh is a separate workflow-gated phase and not unconditional core behavior.

validation_rules: Maintainer amendments must flow through explicit subject_memory refs rather than hidden prompt-only state.
GitHub issue must remain an adapter surface, not the core noun.
assumptions: The non-GitHub fixture may use a synthetic bounded subject because the acceptable source for that fixture remains an open question in the supplied decomposition.
No exact skill registry path or existing template convention under docs/skill-proposals/ was supplied.

execution_plan.objective: Produce separate evidence, findings, and readiness artifacts from a bounded collaboration subject before any publish/build gate proceeds.

policy.stop_condition: Return needs_resolution when rationale is missing, guidance conflicts, or evidence is stale.

follow_on_publication.description: Refreshing the single rolling skill-lab draft PR from the same work ledger is a separate approval-gated follow-on.

steps.description: Normalize the bounded subject around subject_locator, subject_memory, and publication_target, while preserving adapter-specific details inside subject_memory.
Extract the canonical evidence refs and emit a separate canonical evidence manifest.
Produce a maintainer recap and distilled findings without collapsing them into the evidence manifest.
Evaluate rationale completeness, comment consistency, evidence freshness, and approval semantics for the requested publication_target.
Emit the three outputs as separate artifacts even when readiness is blocked, so the result remains inspectable and rebuildable.

open_questions.question: Whether the non-GitHub fixture should remain synthetic or be derived from an existing chat or CLI subject.
Whether docs/skill-proposals/ already has a required naming or template convention.
Whether workflow-gate approval is available for any draft PR refresh follow-on.

adapter_snapshot.title: [skill] Add a collaboration issue distillation skill
adapter_snapshot.objective: Add a governed runx skill proposal for a collaboration issue distillation skill that turns a bounded GitHub issue thread into a reusable approval packet and maintainer recap before any publish or build gate proceeds.
adapter_snapshot.rationale_excerpt: We want issue review to train the operator. Human rationale should become explicit, rebuildable context rather than hidden prompt sprawl.
amendments: hard-cut the core contract to subject_locator, subject_memory, and publication_target
separate canonical evidence, distilled findings, and publish/build readiness
publish_authorization — Refresh the single rolling skill-lab draft PR for this issue from the same work ledger

publication_target.target: single rolling skill-lab draft PR for issue #110

adapter_snapshot.objective: Distill a bounded collaboration review into reusable approval artifacts before publication.

messages.body: Keep the core contract to subject_locator, subject_memory, and publication_target.
Separate canonical evidence, findings, and readiness.
Any publication remains draft-only and approval-gated.

adapter_snapshot.title: [skill] Add a collaboration issue distillation skill
adapter_snapshot.rationale_excerpt: We want issue review to train the operator.

publication_target.target: single rolling skill-lab draft PR for issue #110

acceptance_checks.assertion: The skill requires only subject_locator, subject_memory, and publication_target as top-level inputs; adapter-specific details remain nested under subject_memory.
A GitHub issue thread is treated as one adapter surface and the same chain succeeds for a non-GitHub bounded subject with the same approval semantics.
The chain emits separate canonical_evidence, distilled_findings, and publish_build_readiness outputs rather than one collapsed packet.
Canonical evidence refs are preserved in output and derived context does not replace them.
If the bounded subject lacks explicit rationale, the chain returns needs_resolution and marks publish_build_readiness as blocked for missing_rationale.
If comments or amendments conflict on the governing direction, the chain returns needs_resolution and marks publish_build_readiness as blocked for conflicting_comments.
If subject_memory is older than publication_target.required_evidence_after, the chain returns needs_resolution and marks publish_build_readiness as blocked for stale_evidence.
Successful distillation preserves draft-only publication semantics and does not imply publish authorization; readiness records approval_required when workflow-gated publication is requested.
Maintainer amendments are carried through explicit canonical_evidence_refs or adapter_snapshot content in subject_memory, not hidden prompt-only state.
